title: Deploy Parameter Substitution and Environment Variables
summary: Deploy parameters enable environment-specific value injection (dev/staging/prod) via find-replace, JSONPath key-value, Spark pool, and semantic model binding rules. Values support dynamic variables including $ENV:VAR_NAME for CI/CD secrets injection.

rule_types: find_replace: string replacement in definition payloads (supports regex)
key_value_replace: JSONPath-targeted replacement in JSON definition files
spark_pool: Spark pool instance mapping by environment
semantic_model_binding: semantic model connection ID promotion

dynamic_variables.$workspace.name: Target workspace display name (only when --workspace is passed by name, not ID)
dynamic_variables.$items.Type.Name.id: Deployed GUID of another item in the workspace (e.g., $items.Lakehouse.SalesLH.id)
dynamic_variables.$ENV:VAR_NAME: Value of an OS environment variable (errors if not set)

env_var_injection_example.ci_cd_usage: export DEV_LAKEHOUSE_ID=abc-123 && export PROD_LAKEHOUSE_ID=def-456 && fabio deploy apply --source ./deploy --workspace prod --parameters params.json --env prod

resolution_priority: 1. Exact environment name match (case-insensitive): e.g., 'prod' key
3. Error if no matching environment key found

scoping_rules.item_type: Restrict rule to specific item type(s) — case-insensitive
scoping_rules.item_name: Restrict rule to specific item name(s) — case-insensitive
scoping_rules.file_path: Restrict rule to specific definition file path(s)
scoping_rules.all_optional: If no scoping fields are set, the rule applies globally to all items

init_params_command.scan_mode: fabio deploy init-params --source ./export --out params.json — Scans definitions for GUIDs and generates find_replace template rules
init_params_command.diff_mode: fabio deploy init-params --source ./dev-export --compare ./prod-export --source-env dev --compare-env prod --out params.json — Compares two environments and generates rules with both values pre-filled

key_behaviors.regex_mode: With is_regex: true, only capture group 1 is replaced (surrounding match text preserved). Useful for replacing GUIDs inside larger strings.
key_behaviors.all_fallback: _ALL_ (case-insensitive) acts as universal default when the specific environment name is not found
key_behaviors.creation_payload: Parameter substitution also applies to creationPayload.json (creation-time config), not just definition parts
key_behaviors.hash_recomputation: After substitution, content hashes are recomputed. This ensures idempotent skip detection uses the substituted values.
key_behaviors.error_on_missing_env: If $ENV:VAR_NAME references an unset variable, the deploy fails immediately with INVALID_INPUT error and a hint showing which variable to set

what_agents_should_do: Use fabio deploy init-params to scaffold the parameters file — never write it from scratch
Use $ENV:VAR_NAME for secrets and environment-specific IDs in CI/CD pipelines
Use $workspace.id for self-referential workspace GUIDs (auto-resolved at deploy time)
Use $items.Type.Name.id when one item's definition references another item's deployed GUID
Use _ALL_ as fallback when a value is the same across all environments
Scope rules with item_type/item_name/file_path to avoid unintended replacements in large deployments
Run --dry-run first to verify substitutions produce the expected changeset
